reasoning: As of May 7, 2025, Alberta Premier Danielle Smith has announced that her government will allow a citizen-led referendum on independence 'next year' (2026) or 'sometime in 2026,' provided a petition obtains enough signatures. However, this does not constitute announcing a specific 'date' (which typically requires a day and month). Furthermore, the legislative changes introduced (Bill 54) establish a 120-day window for signature collection, which would not realistically conclude until after the July 1, 2025, deadline for this question. Without the completion of the petition, the government has no trigger or legal basis to officially schedule a precise date for the vote. Political rhetoric from the Premier emphasizes that the government has 'no plans' to put the question forward themselves, further reducing the likelihood of a pre-emptive announcement of a specific date before July.
